title: Search active/archived filter is inverted since v2.3.1 — 'Show active tasks only' returns archived tasks and vice versa
problem: The search filter toggle for active vs. archived tasks is producing inverted results. When 'Show active tasks only' is enabled (the default), search returns archived tasks. When toggled to 'Show archived tasks,' it returns active tasks instead. The underlying task statuses are correct; only the search filter logic is reversed.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3.1 patch update introduced an inversion in the boolean logic that maps the active/archived filter toggle to the search query. Most likely a negation bug (e.g., a flipped conditional, an inverted boolean parameter, or a swapped enum mapping) in the search filter code path changed in that release.
reproduction_steps: Ensure the application is on v2.3.1
Navigate to the main search bar
Leave the default 'Show active tasks only' filter enabled
Search for a keyword that matches both an active task and an archived task (e.g., 'Q2 planning')
Observe that archived tasks appear in results while the known active task is missing
Toggle the filter to 'Show archived tasks'
Observe that the active task now appears in results instead
environment: Application version v2.3.1 (patch update installed ~3 days ago). Reproduced by at least two users. No specific OS/browser constraints reported — appears to be a server-side or shared logic bug.
impact: All users on v2.3.1 who use the default search view see incorrect results. Active tasks are effectively hidden by default, which can cause missed work items and confusion. A workaround exists (manually toggle the filter to the opposite setting), but most users won't discover it without investigation.
recommended_fix: Diff the search filter logic between v2.3.0 and v2.3.1. Look for an inverted boolean condition, negation operator change, or swapped enum/constant in the code that translates the 'active/archived' UI toggle into a query filter. The fix is likely a single-character or single-line boolean inversion.
proposed_test_case: Unit test: given the 'Show active tasks only' filter is enabled, assert that the search query filters to status=active (not status=archived), and vice versa. Integration test: create one active and one archived task with the same keyword, search with each filter setting, and verify the correct task appears in each case.
information_gaps: Exact code change in v2.3.1 that introduced the inversion has not been identified yet
Whether the bug affects API-level search or only the UI search bar is unknown
Full scope of affected users/instances beyond the two confirmed reporters
